name: Age-Restricted Products
description: Campaigns for products with legal minimum age requirements (alcohol, tobacco, gambling, cannabis). Triggers age-gating, time-of-day restrictions, and content placement rules.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US TTB Alcohol Advertising
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau regulations. No targeting of audiences where more than 28.4% are under legal drinking age.

regulatory_frameworks.name: UK ASA Alcohol Rules
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Advertising Standards Authority. Alcohol ads must not appeal to under-18s or be placed in media where 25%+ of the audience is under 18.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US State Gambling Laws
regulatory_frameworks.summary: State-by-state gambling advertising regulations. Geo-fencing to legal jurisdictions, self-exclusion list compliance, responsible gambling messaging.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US State Cannabis Laws
regulatory_frameworks.summary: State-level cannabis advertising rules. Typically require geo-restriction to legal states, age-gating, and health warnings.

guidance: Age restriction rules vary significantly by jurisdiction and product type. Alcohol age is 21 in the US, 18 in the UK/EU. Gambling legality varies by US state. Cannabis is federally illegal in the US but permitted in some states. Governance agents must cross-reference plan.countries and plan.regions with product-specific age and legality requirements.
